place: Woodcliff Lake borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Woodcliff Lake became a borough on August 31 1894, part of Bergen County’s Boroughitis boom that saw 26 new boroughs spring up that year.
On March 1 1910 the borough renamed itself Woodcliff Lake to match the post office after the reservoir was built.
The Woodcliff Reservoir, largely inside the borough, dictates the town’s layout by limiting central density and preserving wooded buffers.
Before Europeans, the Lenape Tappan and Hackensack peoples farmed, hunted, and fished the fertile Pascack Valley that now hosts Woodcliff Lake.
Most Woodcliff Lake lots are at least 22,500 sq ft, a zoning rule that keeps the borough’s streets lined with spacious homes.
Van Riper’s Farm, founded in the late 1700s, was famed for its apple cider and an annual turkey shoot that drew regional visitors.
